channel.title: Origin-Story on zhouyang — The 25th Frame
channel.description: Recent content in Origin-Story on zhouyang — The 25th Frame

item.title: Longjing: One Leaf, One Spring
item.description: The first time I saw Longjing leaves hit hot water, I was twenty-three and standing in a tea house on the southern shore of West Lake. The tea master — a woman in her seventies with hands that looked carved from cypress — poured from a long-spout kettle in a single continuous arc. The leaves didn’t float. They stood upright. One by one, pale green needles rose from the bottom of the glass like a forest coming awake. It was February. The air outside was forty degrees. Inside, the glass was seventy degrees and smelled like roasted chestnuts and fresh-cut grass and something else I couldn’t name. I asked her what the something else was.

item.title: The Moutai Puzzle: Why a Bottle Costs More Than Your Graphics Card
item.description: The Chishui River flows through Guizhou province the color of strong tea — red-brown with iron-rich silt, warm even in December. On both banks, for about fifteen kilometers, sit the distilleries that produce the most valuable spirit brand on Earth. Moutai’s market cap is larger than Diageo, the company that owns Johnnie Walker, Guinness, and Tanqueray combined. A single bottle of their Flying Fairy can cost more than an RTX 4090.
